understanding.project_summary: This project provides a Wardrobe Recommendation Engine implemented in Python. It builds metadata and embeddings for wardrobe images, supports hybrid text+visual retrieval, offers reranking with LLMs, stores vectors and conversation memory in PostgreSQL/pgvector, and includes CLI scripts and demos to run ingestion, searches and an interactive chat assistant.
understanding.architecture_overview: The code is organized into utility, ingestion/captioning, embedding, retrieval, reranking, recommender and persistence layers. Utilities (src.utils) provide filesystem helpers. Captioner (src.engine.captioner) turns images into captions/metadata; embeddings (src.engine.embeddings and src.engine.visual_embeddings) create text and visual embeddings and produce hybrid items. Hybrid retrieval and similar-search modules (src.engine.hybrid_retrieval, src.engine.similar_search, src.engine.pgvector_similar_search) perform nearest-neighbour scoring using either in-file hybrid JSON or PostgreSQL-backed vectors (src.db.pgvector_store). LLM-based rerankers (src.engine.llm_reranker, src.engine.llm_similar_reranker) refine candidate lists. Outfit construction and presentation are handled by outfit_recommender and fashion_assistant modules. Chat memory is persisted through a Postgres-backed adapter (src.db.postgres_chat_memory) with a local adapter wrapper (src.engine.chat_memory). Top-level scripts and main.py orchestrate setup, data-building, migrations and demo/chat runs.

execution_flow.title: Initialize environment and assets
execution_flow.description: Run main() to validate environment variables, ensure required data folders and uploaded images exist, and print status headers. main calls check_environment and check_uploaded_images to verify prerequisites.

execution_flow.title: Build local metadata and embeddings (optional)
execution_flow.description: If metadata/embedding JSON files are missing, captioner.process_inventory creates wardrobe metadata, embeddings.generate_embeddings_from_metadata creates text embeddings, and visual_embeddings.generate_visual_and_hybrid_embeddings adds visual embeddings to produce hybrid items.

execution_flow.title: Prepare PostgreSQL/pgvector
execution_flow.description: main checks DB connectivity via src.db.pgvector_store.get_item_count and can create pgvector schema with create_pgvector_schema. If DB is empty, migrate_to_pgvector_if_needed inserts hybrid JSON items into pgvector using upsert_wardrobe_item.

execution_flow.title: Start demos / API / interactive chat
execution_flow.description: Scripts and main() provide entrypoints to run demos and the interactive chatbot. scripts.run_api exists as a script entry; fashion assistant demos and fashion_chat_demo start conversational flows that call FashionChatAssistant.chat.

execution_flow.title: Retrieve candidates (hybrid or pgvector)
execution_flow.description: Search flows use either in-file hybrid retrieval (src.engine.hybrid_retrieval / src.engine.similar_search) or DB-backed retrieval (src.db.pgvector_store). Hybrid_search and similar_search functions compute text and visual similarities and return ranked candidates.

execution_flow.title: Rerank and build outfit recommendations
execution_flow.description: Candidate pools are optionally reranked with LLM-based rerankers (src.engine.llm_reranker, src.engine.llm_similar_reranker). Outfit recommendations are created and validated by src.engine.outfit_recommender and formatted/presented by src.engine.fashion_assistant or FashionChatAssistant for chat responses.

risks_and_gaps.title: Authentication and Access Control
risks_and_gaps.description: No authentication, authorization or API endpoint contracts were discovered. If the project is exposed as a service, access controls and secrets handling (API keys / DB credentials) are not documented in code.

risks_and_gaps.title: No HTTP API surface documented
risks_and_gaps.description: main.py is described as an application entrypoint and FastAPI startup module but the scan did not expose any HTTP routes or OpenAPI schema. The project relies on CLI scripts and demos; an HTTP contract is missing.

risks_and_gaps.title: Error handling and retries
risks_and_gaps.description: Many integration points (DB, external LLM/embedding calls, CLIP model) are present but explicit retry logic and robust error handling are not visible in the scanned functions.

risks_and_gaps.title: Observability and logging
risks_and_gaps.description: There is no centralized logging, metrics, or tracing instrumentation visible. Missing observability makes debugging production failures and performance issues difficult.

risks_and_gaps.title: Configuration and secrets
risks_and_gaps.description: Environment variables and configuration checks are referenced (check_environment), but configuration documentation and secure secret management are not provided in code. .env.example exists but contents were not included.

risks_and_gaps.title: Testing and CI
risks_and_gaps.description: The project scan did not reveal unit or integration tests, test harnesses, or CI configuration. Core modules interacting with external services would benefit from tests and mocks.

risks_and_gaps.title: Data persistence and migration clarity
risks_and_gaps.description: There is migration logic from JSON files to pgvector, but details about idempotency, large dataset handling, and rollback on failures are not fully documented.

recommended_document_sections.title: Quickstart: Run the demo
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Step-by-step instructions to install requirements, populate .env, start PostgreSQL/pgvector (Docker), build metadata/embeddings and run the interactive chat/demo scripts.

recommended_document_sections.title: Data model and JSON formats
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe wardrobe_inventory.json, wardrobe_metadata.json, wardrobe_embeddings.json and wardrobe_hybrid_embeddings.json schemas so contributors can prepare or extend datasets.

recommended_document_sections.title: Database setup and migration
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain DATABASE_URL usage, how create_pgvector_schema works, migration flow (migrate_json_to_pgvector), idempotency guarantees and required extensions (pgvector).

recommended_document_sections.title: Search & retrieval flows
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document the retrieval paths (in-file hybrid, pgvector-backed), weighting formulas, and how to choose between hybrid_retrieval, similar_search and pgvector search modules.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM usage and prompts
recommended_document_sections.purpose: List LLM models used, where LLMs are called (captioner, rerankers, outfit LLMs), expected prompt behavior, token limits and cost considerations.

recommended_document_sections.title: Operational considerations
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance for production: secrets management, logging/monitoring suggestions, scaling pgvector, model caching, and hardware requirements for visual embedding (CLIP).

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing & CI
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Suggested tests (unit, integration, mocked LLM/DB), CI pipeline steps and example test cases for core modules like pgvector_store, captioner and recommender logic.
